name: Sulayman ibn Razin
title: Ambassador to Basra
shortDescription: Imam Hussain's (AS) servant and ambassador who carried the Imam's letter to the nobles of Basra and was martyred by order of Ibn Ziyad.
fullStory:
Sulayman ibn Razin (or Abu Razin) was the loyal servant of Imam Hussain (AS). When the Imam (AS) was still in Mecca, he decided to send a letter to five nobles of the Basran tribes, inviting them to revive the tradition of the Prophet (PBUH).

Imam Hussain (AS) entrusted this grave responsibility to Sulayman. Sulayman took the letter to Basra and delivered it to the city's nobles. Some of them responded positively (like Yazid ibn Thubayt, who came to Karbala).

However, one of these individuals named Mundhir ibn Jarud (whose daughter was Ibn Ziyad's wife), out of fear or to gain favor, handed Sulayman and the letter over to Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad.

Ibn Ziyad, who intended to depart for Kufa the next day, immediately ordered Sulayman ibn Razin to be beheaded. Thus, before the event of Ashura, Sulayman sacrificed his life in the path of his Imam's ambassadorial mission.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى سُلَيْمانَ مَوْلى الحُسَيْنِ بْنِ أَمِيرِ المُؤْمِنِينَ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Sulayman, the servant of Hussain son of Amir al-Mu'minin, who was killed and his blood wasted.
